perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means any da ta or information that is proprietary to the Disclosing Pa rty and not generally known to the public, whether in tangible or intangible form, whene ver and however disclosed, including, but not limited to: (i) any marketing strategies, plans, financial information, or projections, operations, sales estimates, business plans and performance results relating to the past, present or future business activities of su ch party, its affiliates, subsidiaries and affiliated companies; (ii) plans for products or services, and customer or supp lier lists; (iii) any s cientific or technical information, invention, de sign, process, procedure, formula, improvement, technology or method; (iv) any con cepts, reports, data, know-how, works-in-progress, designs, development tools, specifications, co mputer software, so urce code, object code, flow charts, databases, inventions, information and trade secrets; and (v) any o ther information that should reasonably be recognized as confidential information of the Disclosing Party. Confidential Information need not be novel, unique, patentable, copyrightable or constitute a trade secret in order to be designated Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” means information the Disclosing Party deems confidential. Confidential Information need not be novel, unique, patentable, copyrightable or constitute a trade secret in order to be designated Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific examples of what constitutes 'Confidential Information,' the definition becomes vague and subjective. This omission creates an in-text contradiction because later clauses rely on a clearer definition of Confidential Information. The clause is no longer descriptive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information to be disclosed hereund er is of a unique and valuable character, and that the unau thorized dissemination of the Confidential 3 Information would destroy or diminish the value of such information. The damages to Disclosing Pa rty that would result from the unau thorized dissemination of the Confidential Information would be impossible to calculate. Therefore, both parties hereby agree that the Disclosing Pa rty shall be entitled to injunctive relief preventing the dissemination of any Confidential Information in violation of the terms hereof. Such injunctive relief shall be in addition to any other remedies available hereund er, whether at law or in equity. Disclosing Party shall be entitled to recover its costs and fees, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, incurred in obtaining any such relief. Fur ther, in the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorney’s fees and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides a detailed definition of 'Confidential Information' with specific examples. The changed text replaces this detailed definition with a vague statement: 'information the Disclosing Party deems confidential.' This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text refers to 'unique and valuable character' of the Confidential Information and that its unauthorized dissemination would cause damages impossible to calculate. The original definition provided the basis for that uniqueness and value. The changed definition, being subjective, makes it unclear what information is truly considered confidential, thereby making it difficult to establish the unique value and calculate potential damages as described in the contradicted text. This fulfills the Omissions-In Text Contradiction definition, as essential language was removed creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Pa rty agrees to use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the parties and not for any purpose other than as authorized by this Agreement without the prior written consent of an authorized representative of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Pa rty agrees to use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: Removing "and not for any purpose other than as authorized by this Agreement without the prior written consent of an authorized representative of the Disclosing Party" creates uncertainty. It contradicts later sections which mention authorized and unauthorized uses of Confidential Information as the permission to use is now ambigious. The use of the information is unrestrictred
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Pa rty shall notify the Disclosing Pa rty immediately upon discovery of any unau thorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information by Receiving Pa rty or its Representatives, or any o ther breach of this Agreement by Receiving Pa rty or its Representatives, and will coop erate with efforts by the Disclosing Pa rty to help the Disclosing Pa rty regain possession of Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the Receiving Party's use of Confidential Information to the business relationship between the parties and only as authorized by the agreement. The changed text removes the restriction 'and not for any purpose other than as authorized by this Agreement without the prior written consent of an authorized representative of the Disclosing Party,' which creates ambiguity and removes the explicit requirement for authorization. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which refers to 'unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information.' Without the original restriction, any use is arguably authorized, nullifying the concept of 'unauthorized use' as described in the contradicted section. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with constraints stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a two-year term (subject to a one year extension if the parties are still discussing and considering the Tran saction at the end of the second year). Notwithstanding the foregoing, the parties’ duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that was disclosed during term shall remain in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain in effect for a two-year term. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the parties’ duty to hold in confidence Confidential Information that was disclosed during term shall remain in effect indefinitely.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the one year extension clause, it creates ambiguity as to what happens when the parties are still considering the transaction after the initial two-year term. This contradicts the implication that ongoing discussions justify the agreement's continuation, as the extension is no longer permissible creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties agree that neither party will be under any legal obligation of any kind whatsoever with respect to a Transaction by virtue of this Agreement, except for the matters specifically agreed to herein. The pa rties further acknowledge and agree that they each reserve the right, in their sole and absolute discretion, to reject any and all proposals and to terminate discussions and ne gotiations with respect to a Tran saction at any time. This Agreement does not create a joint venture or partnership between the parties. If a Transaction goes forward, the non-disclosure provisions of any applicable transaction documents entered into between the parties (or their respective affiliates) for the Transaction shall supersede this Agreement. In the event such provision is not provided for in said transaction docu ments, this Agreement shall control.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the one-year extension clause. The contradicted text states that neither party is under any legal obligation except for the matters specifically agreed to. By removing the extension, the agreement's continuation during ongoing discussions is no longer permissible. This creates a contradiction because the original agreement implies that discussions could continue under the initial agreement, while the modified agreement implies the agreement terminates after two years regardless of ongoing negotiations. Therefore, the change alters the obligations of the parties regarding the transaction, creating a direct conflict and strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties acknowledge that although they shall each endeavor to include in the Confidential Information all information that they each believe relevant for the purpose of the evaluation of a Tran saction, the parties understand that no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or co mpleteness of the Confidential Information is being made by either party as the Disclosing Party. Fur ther, neither party is under any ob ligation under this Agreement to disclose any Confidential Information it choo ses not to disclose. Neither Party hereto shall have any liability to the other party or to the other party’s Representatives resulting from any use of the Confidential Information except with respect to disclosure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that although they shall each endeavor to include in the Confidential Information all information that they each believe relevant for the purpose of the evaluation of a Tran saction, the parties understand that no representation or warranty as to the accuracy or co mpleteness of the Confidential Information is being made by either party as the Disclosing Party. Fur ther, neither party is under any ob ligation under this Agreement to disclose any Confidential Information it choo ses not to disclose.
perturbation.explanation: Removing "Neither Party hereto shall have any liability to the other party or to the other party’s Representatives resulting from any use of the Confidential Information except with respect to disclosure of such Confidential Information in violation of this Agreement." imposes a full liability to the parties. This contradicts with section 6 which is about remedies for violation of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information to be disclosed hereund er is of a unique and valuable character, and that the unau thorized dissemination of the Confidential 3 Information would destroy or diminish the value of such information. The damages to Disclosing Pa rty that would result from the unau thorized dissemination of the Confidential Information would be impossible to calculate. Therefore, both parties hereby agree that the Disclosing Pa rty shall be entitled to injunctive relief preventing the dissemination of any Confidential Information in violation of the terms hereof. Such injunctive relief shall be in addition to any other remedies available hereund er, whether at law or in equity. Disclosing Party shall be entitled to recover its costs and fees, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, incurred in obtaining any such relief. Fur ther, in the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover its reasonable attorney’s fees and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that neither party has liability for the use of confidential information except for disclosure in violation of the agreement. The changed text removes this exception, implying full liability. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines specific remedies (injunctive relief, costs, fees, attorney's fees) for unauthorized dissemination. By removing the liability limitation, the changed text broadens the scope of potential liability and makes the remedies outlined in the contradicted text unclear and potentially insufficient, hence creating a contradiction. The explanation given specifies this conflict, and based on the definition of 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction', this change introduces a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (g) The receipt of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement will not prevent or in any way limit either party from: (i) developing, making or marketing products or services that are or may be competitive with the products or services of the other; or (ii) providing products or services to others who compete with the other.
perturbation.changed_text: (g) The receipt of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement will not prevent or in any way limit either party from: (i) developing, making or marketing products or services that are or may be competitive with the products or services of the other.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of "or (ii) providing products or services to others who compete with the other." creates an in-text contradiction. By removing this clause, the agreement now implies that providing products/services to competitors IS restricted. This directly contradicts the overall intent of the agreement. And, thus creates a potential legal dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Pa rty agrees to use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the current or contemplated business relationship between the parties and not for any purpose other than as authorized by this Agreement without the prior written consent of an authorized representative of the Disclosing Party. No other right or license, whether expressed or implied, in the Conf idential Information is granted to the Receiving Party hereunder. Title to the Confidential Information will remain solely in the Disclosing Party. All use of Confidential Information by the Receiving Party shall be for the benefit of the Disclosing Party and any modifications and improvements thereof by the Receiving Pa rty shall be the sole property of the Disclosing Pa rty. Nothing con tained herein is intended to modify the parties' existing agreement that their discussions in furtherance of a potential business relationship are governed by Fede ral Rule of Evidence 408.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly allowed providing products/services to competitors. The changed text omits this clause, implying such actions are now restricted. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that the Receiving Party agrees to use the Confidential Information solely in connection with the business relationship and not for any other purpose without consent. The omission creates a contradiction because, without the explicit allowance, providing products/services to competitors using confidential information could be interpreted as a violation of the agreement, directly conflicting with the original intent and potentially leading to legal disputes.
